title: KV Schooling — Kendriya Vidyalaya
description: Kendriya Vidyalaya, central government schooling. Science and math stream through Class 12.

title: Enrolled at MIT-WPU, Computer Engineering
description: Started B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering at MIT World Peace University, Pune.

title: Peer Tutor — Maths, C++, Python, ML, R
description: Tutored batchmates across Maths I/II/III, C++, Python, R, Tableau, and intro ML. Started because i loved teaching and people asked and kept going because it forced me to understand the material properly.

title: Freelancer — Software & Web Projects
description: Started picking up software and web projects alongside college. Ran mostly on referrals. The Trek Recommendation System was the last built during this stretch.

title: Yale, Wesleyan & Duke — Psychology Certifications
description: Completed Introduction to Psychology (Yale), Social Psychology with Honors (Wesleyan), and Business Metrics for Data-Driven Companies (Duke) during COVID lockdown. The beginning of behavioral research.

title: Domino's Data Leak — Behavior Tracing Catalyst
description: Domino's India data breach exposed 13 crore consumer records. Sparked behavior tracing as a formal research direction — mapping what data reveals about people, not just what people say.

title: RA — Data Warehouse Lab, MIT-WPU
description: Prof. Varsha reached out after a semester project on psychological market basket analysis caught her attention. Joined the Data Warehouse Lab as Research Assistant and spent the next 30 months running behavioral research across 10,000+ parameters.

title: HelpingWind — Social Media & Content
description: First formal work role. Managed social media and content writing for HelpingWind. Covered SEO, web content, and digital marketing.

title: Haar Cascade Research — Smart Voting System
description: Read through 14 published studies on face recognition methods. The question was which hybrid would actually work for single-voter, real-time deployment. Built the Haar Cascade + LBPH pipeline in Python and OpenCV.

title: Open Research Forum — 1,000+ Peer Interviews
description: Set up a research forum to run video interviews. Over 1,000 conversations with students across different academic backgrounds, all mapped to decision-making patterns. The raw data became the foundation for three published papers.

title: Conversational Bot — T5 Model + Skinner's CBT
description: Built 'Speak It Out', a multilingual bot using T5 grounded in Skinner's CBT framework. It reads emotional and linguistic patterns in user input and returns a behavioral report. Submitted to SIH for funding.

title: Behavioral Psychology Paper — Springer IACC
description: Published ML-based behavioral clustering across 1,438 respondents. Five behavioral clusters identified; 41% 'merciful-silent' type. COVID shifted cultural values — family rose above success. CCIS vol. 1528.

title: Face Recognition Paper — IEEE Smart GENCON
description: Presented at IEEE Smart GENCON 2021, Pune. LBPH achieved 93.33% accuracy in the wearable-device domain, outperforming FisherFace. Single-face model validated for Smart Voting deployment.

title: TechHut — Full-Stack Developer Intern
description: Built a MERN application for real-time cryptocurrency price tracking. Implemented filtering API with MongoDB, built the React frontend.

title: AI / ML Freelancer — Active Period
description: Active freelance stretch handling NLP, ML, and data pipeline work for independent clients.

title: IdeaIn10 Runner-Up — IET Pune, 'Speak It Out'
description: Secured second place at IET Pune Chapter Ideathon for Speak It Out — a conversational empathy model. Recognition led to university funding pursuit via Smart India Hackathon.

title: AI Research Intern — C-DAC Pune
description: Final Year Project sponsored by C-DAC Applied AI Group (Strategic Projects Group, Ministry of Electronics & IT, Govt. of India), under Dr. Krishnanjan Bhattacharjee (Joint Director, SPG). Researched Domain Identification for Named Entity Disambiguation using DistilBERT + Zero-Shot classification across 6 domains on an 11,000+ dataset. Achieved 99.24% domain classification accuracy and 93% NER. Built an autonomous data pipeline and a Streamlit web interface.

title: SIH Grant — INR ₹1,00,000 for Speak It Out
description: Smart India Hackathon 2022: presented Speak It Out. Secured a Government of India innovation grant of INR 1,00,000 to further develop the conversational empathy model.

title: Student Peace Ambassador — MIT-WPU · UNESCO
description: Appointed Student Peace Ambassador and Speaker at the 8th World Parliament of Science, Religion, and Philosophy — MIT-WPU in association with UNESCO.

title: Nutritional Psychology Paper — Springer Nature
description: Published ML-based nutritional psychology research with the Biology department. Decision Tree on sickness frequency: 98%. Random Forest on BMI: 96.53%. LNEE vol. 1066.

title: Trek Recommendation System — Freelance Project
description: Delivered the Trek Recommendation System as a freelance project. 208+ Indian treks, content-based filtering with scikit-learn KNN, Flask REST API, under a second on lookup. First ML system I shipped after college.

title: Synpulse Internship — BRv7 Certified
description: Interned at Synpulse Management Consulting, Pune. Built a dashboard using Angular, Spring Boot, SQL, and Python for the Business Radar project. Earned BRv7 certification and secured a Pre-Placement Offer.

title: Joined Synpulse — Trainee Analyst
description: Joined Synpulse full-time as Trainee Analyst. Designed a mutual fund system design for a leading private bank and got a Pat on the Back award for it. Recognized as Star Performer for building the data pipeline and UI for Syninformation platform. One of 6 selected across India for Synpulse's internal data analytics certification.

title: Synpulse — Promoted to Analyst
description: Promoted to Analyst. Took on institutional client consulting for a global bank, mostly ICM systems design and front-office automated system implementation. Moved to a Southeast Asian core banking project as a backend and infrastructure engineer.

title: IoB Consumer Psychology — Taylor & Francis
description: Published Internet of Behaviors framework. Adults 20–40 drove 72.36% of orders. Instagram averaged 75% engagement. Users spending >2 hours were 45.08% buy-influenced vs 38.53% for <1 hour.

title: E-commerce Behavioral Framework
description: Took the IoB survey data further. Segmented by platform and demographic, mapped Diwali in November as a 13.64% annual order peak, and traced how gender shapes discovery versus purchase intent.

title: ONCP Vision — Protocol for Content Distribution
description: The ONCP concept came from watching my father's cable business for years. Institutions didn't outcompete LCOs, they bypassed them by owning the entire stack. ONCP is a concept response to that: a five-layer DPI protocol that gives LCOs an infrastructure role rather than asking them to compete at the application layer.

title: Synpulse — Promoted to Senior Analyst
description: Promoted to Senior Analyst. Backend and infrastructure engineering at the core, but the role operates across multiple interface levels — system design, client consulting, and delivery. Working across 3 projects under one client simultaneously, mostly in financial systems for global banks and private wealth clients.

title: ONCP Architecture Refined
description: Fleshed out the five layers: Open Content Network API, Shared National CDN, Device-Neutral OS, Aadhaar+UPI identity, and edge compute. The argument is that LCOs aren't dumb pipes. They're infrastructure nodes that lack a protocol to operate as one. That's the gap ONCP is designed to fill.

title: Financial Analysis Engine — Personal Market Research
description: Built a RAG pipeline for financial metric extraction applied to personal market analysis. Exploration of the psychology and trading intersection. Private work — no public trace.

title: Sansad — Indian Election Strategy Game (WIP)
description: Building a browser strategy game set in the 2024 Indian General Election. 543 constituencies, 5,000+ candidates, an AI advisor that comments on every move, 7-turn campaign. Closest I've got to the intersection of politics, data, and game design.

title: TraceAI — Agentic Research Platform (Active)
description: Building TraceAI. It maps research as an ontology graph so concepts, hypotheses, evidence, and issues all connect explicitly. Powered by Groq LLaMA 3.3 70B with per-node reasoning threads, voice input via Whisper, and a live ReactFlow canvas. The point is the AI can't drift from what you've actually built.
